mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks such as text generation, audio, and image processing without requiring a GPU. Ideal for users seeking a local-first solution with decentralized inference capabilities.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for managing LocalAI deployments.

```bash

curl -Lo localai https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai

sudo mv localai /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, which is suitable for local development and testing.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Start Minikube with specified resources.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> Set the current context to Minikube.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI to your Kubernetes cluster using a Helm chart.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI in the localai namespace.

steps.title: Verify LocalAI Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks if the LocalAI pods are running successfully in the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List all pods in the localai namespace to verify deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes LocalAI from your Kubernetes cluster and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall LocalAI from the specified namespace.
> Delete the namespace to remove all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
Backup your current deployment, upgrade to the latest version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all -n localai -o yaml > localai-backup.yaml

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> Backup current LocalAI resources.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n localai -o yaml > localai-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Docker image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available on Docker Hub or your registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your Kubernetes cluster is running and accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated to run LocalAI.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource allocation for your Minikube cluster with 'minikube stop' and 'minikube start --cpus=4 --memory=8192'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved performance for existing models.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n localai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
